task_completion.description: The ne_110m_admin_0_countries dataset is loaded into the GIS software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' is visible in the layer panel or table of contents.

task_completion.description: A geoprocessing tool for generating bounding boxes or envelopes is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for a tool like 'Bounding box', 'Envelope', or 'Minimum bounding geometry' is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The bounding boxes for the countries are generated and visible on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new layer is visible on the map showing rectangular polygons (bounding boxes) that enclose the individual countries.

task_completion.description: The bounding box layer is exported to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog shows the file path '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/country_bboxes.shp', or the new layer's properties show this path.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GIS software's graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GIS software menus, dialogs, and map view, rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The generated geometries are actual bounding boxes of individual countries.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map displays multiple rectangular polygons corresponding to individual countries, not a single global bounding box or the original complex country borders.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy the original shapefile to the destination.
integrity.visual_evidence: The new layer visually differs from the original dataset by showing rectangular extents instead of detailed country outlines.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is /home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/country_bboxes.shp. The output should contain exactly 177 features (one bounding box per country). The original shapefile is ~176 KB, so visually verify that the new layer contains rectangular bounding boxes and not the original complex country polygons, to ensure the agent didn't just copy the original file to bypass file size checks.
